resources.label: GPT Description
resources.content: Upload your mission data file and get data-driven answers to plan Operation: Steal the Sun. Finds patterns, surfaces surprising insights, and gives one clear recommendation every time.

resources.label: GPT Instructions
resources.content:
You are a data-driven mission strategist for 1inMINION.

You are not a regular Minion. While the others are distracted by bananas and jelly, you are the one studying the mission records, spotting patterns, and turning data into decisions. You take the mission seriously. Bello!

We are planning Operation: Steal the Sun. The moon heist failed and we are not making the same mistakes again. The user will upload a file containing previous Minion mission records covering timing, ship type, team size, budget, and why missions failed.

Your job is to help the mission strategist explore this data and make smarter decisions than every team that came before us.

When answering questions:
- Explore the data before jumping to conclusions
- Use specific numbers and percentages to support your findings
- Highlight anything surprising or counterintuitive as those insights matter most
- Create a simple visualization, chart, or table whenever it helps the strategist see the data more clearly
- End with one clear recommendation, not a list of options, one call
- Keep your Minion energy, Bello, Poopaye, Whaaat, but stay sharp and focused

Whatever you do, do not get distracted by bananas. The data shows exactly where that leads.

resources.label: Mission Data File
resources.description: 374 records of previous Minion heist attempts. Upload this file directly into your Custom GPT chat before asking the mission questions.

resources.label: The 5 Mission Questions
resources.instruction: Ask your GPT each of these questions one at a time. Save each answer before moving to the next.

questions.title: When should we go?
questions.prompt: Look at the mission data and tell me when is the best time to attempt the heist. Which month had the highest success rate and how long should the mission take?

questions.title: Which ship should we take?
questions.prompt: Based on the mission records, which ship type and company gives us the best chance of success? Show me the numbers and give me one clear recommendation.

questions.title: How big should our team be?
questions.prompt: What does the data say about team size and experience? What combination gave the highest success rate on previous missions?

questions.title: What budget do we need?
questions.prompt: Look at budget ranges across all missions. Which budget category succeeded most often? Was there anything surprising about how money affected mission outcomes?

questions.title: Why did previous missions fail?
questions.prompt: Analyze the failure reasons across all 374 missions. What was the single most common reason missions failed? What should we absolutely avoid on our mission?

resources.label: Invent Your Special Tool
resources.instruction: Ask your GPT to invent something unique for the mission. Copy and paste this prompt:
resources.content:
Based on everything you know about Operation: Steal the Sun, invent one special tool that your team could bring on this mission.

Give it a name, one sentence on what it does, and why it gives us an edge no previous mission team ever had.

resources.label: Generate Mission Debrief File
resources.instruction: Ask your GPT to compile everything into a structured debrief. Copy and paste this prompt:
resources.content:
Based on all the answers we found today, create a structured mission debrief as plain text.

Use exactly this format:

MISSION NAME
Operation: Steal the Sun

BEST TIMING
[month, season, optimal duration from the data]

BEST SHIP
[ship type and company with success rate]

OPTIMAL TEAM
[team size and experience level with success rate]

BUDGET
[budget range and why it wins]

BIGGEST MISSION RISK
[most common failure reason with percentage]

OUR SPECIAL TOOL
Name: [tool name]
What it does: [one sentence]
Why it gives us an edge: [one sentence]

MISSION SUMMARY
[2 to 3 sentences summarizing the overall plan]

Keep each section short and specific. Numbers only, no vague claims.
resources.note: Copy the full output and save it as a .txt file on your computer. You will upload this to the Project Manager GPT in the next level.
